mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) when controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity, forcing firms to cut margins and driving negative monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of tightening periods with falling INDPRO, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% signals restrictive monetary conditions that compress equity valuations, producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls alongside a stable unemployment rate indicates healthy job creation without wage-price spirals, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over individual series in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations, generating positive monthly S&P 500 returns through fundamental earnings channels.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply chain bottlenecks, triggering immediate risk-off flows and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of instances where INDPRO drops >0.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.05) in walk-forward testing.
